trade.summary: The United States is Israel's largest trading partner. The US-Israel Free Trade Agreement (1985) was the first FTA the US signed with any country. Bilateral trade spans technology, pharmaceuticals, diamonds, and defense. US tech giants (Intel, Google, Microsoft, Apple) have major R&D centers in Israel. Israel is among the top recipients of US foreign military financing.

diplomatic.summary: The US-Israel relationship is one of the most consequential bilateral partnerships in the world. The US provides Israel with $3.8 billion in annual military aid under a 10-year MOU. The relationship was tested by disagreements over Gaza policy under the Biden administration but remained fundamentally intact. The Trump administration returned to office in 2025 and adopted an even more strongly pro-Israel posture, supporting Israeli security operations and moving to further strengthen the alliance.
recent_incidents: US vetoed multiple UN ceasefire resolutions 2023-2024
US provided emergency military aid after October 7
US deployed carrier strike groups to deter regional escalation

military.summary: The closest military relationship Israel has with any country. The US provides $3.8 billion annually in Foreign Military Financing. Israel is a major non-NATO ally and has access to advanced US weapons systems. The two countries conduct joint exercises, share intelligence, and coordinate on regional security. The US deployed two carrier strike groups to the Eastern Mediterranean after October 7, 2023. The Iron Dome and other Israeli missile defense systems were co-developed with US funding.

financial.summary: Massive US investment in Israeli tech ecosystem. US venture capital dominates Israeli startup funding. US tech giants have major Israeli R&D operations. Israeli companies widely listed on NASDAQ and NYSE. The US provides both military and economic assistance.

energy.summary: Growing bilateral energy linkages as Israel develops its offshore gas fields. US energy companies involved in Israeli offshore development. Strategic alignment on energy security in the Eastern Mediterranean region.

scientific.summary: Among the strongest bilateral S&T relationships in the world. The US-Israel Binational Science Foundation and Binational Industrial R&D Foundation support hundreds of joint projects annually. Joint research spans defense, biotech, AI, semiconductors, and climate. Israeli researchers have deep ties to US universities.
